response:
To test this function effectively, we should use `pytest.mark.parametrize`. This allows us to test multiple inputs against the same logic cleanly, separating the "Happy Path" (valid emails), "Edge Cases" (tricky but valid/invalid formats), and "Error Cases" (invalid types).

Here is the complete test suite:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_valid_email  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual filename

@pytest.mark.parametrize("valid_email", [
    "simple@example.com",
    "very.common@example.com",
    "disposable.style.email.with+symbol@example.com",
    "other.email-with-hyphen@example.com",
    "fully-qualified-domain@example.cluster.local",
    "user.name+tag+sorting@example.com",
    "1234567890@example.com",
    "x@example.com",
    "example-indeed@strange-example.com
